description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with a striking blue fuselage and wings, adorned with gold decorative motifs on the upper wing surfaces. The aircraft features two sets of straight, parallel wings connected by struts and wires, typical of early 20th-century designs. Its nose houses a wooden propeller and a radial engine cowling, while the tail section is rounded with horizontal and vertical stabilizers. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and exposed. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, likely accommodating a single pilot. The overall construction appears to be primarily metal and wood, with fabric-covered wings and tail surfaces.
description: A blue and gold vintage biplane with dual wings, open cockpit, wooden propeller, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the blue fuselage, gold propeller, open cockpit, and decorative gold motifs on the upper wing.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tail section, twin wings, and the strut-and-wire bracing between the wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the propeller, cockpit, and tail details.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the wooden propeller, engine cowling, and the symmetrical biplane wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings, no winglets, and visible strut-and-wire bracing.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, mid-fuselage.
